rationale: Live-action UGC testimonial framing clashes hard with Helix Jump's character-free, neon 3D minimalist DNA — there's no on-screen avatar or narrative protagonist to anchor a "you look happier" hook, and the muted sky-blue/black/white member palette undercuts the high-contrast neon that makes Helix gameplay legible. The hook also fails to showcase the rotate-and-fall core loop, which is the proven conversion driver for hypercasual arcade. Audience overlap (broad, casual mobile gamers) is the only real bridge; if tested, restrict to a short UGC cold-open that hard-cuts into authentic gameplay capture with score/level overlays within 2 seconds.
